title: euphorbia cornigera
intro: Euphorbia cornigera, also known as the Horned Spurge or Horned Euphorbia, is a succulent plant native to tropical Africa, characterized by its distinctive horn-like protrusions on its stem. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Euphorbia cornigera have been linked to several health benefits, including the potential treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, Euphorbia cornigera is often used in the form of a decoction, where the plant is steeped in hot water to release its active compounds, or as a tincture, where the plant is extracted in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin.
